item.title: Silent catch block in gateway/health.ts hides connection errors
item.intent: Gateway health check failures must log the underlying error. When the agent goes down, the operator needs the actual error (connection refused, timeout, auth failure) — not just 'down'.
item.description: src/gateway/health.ts line 104 has a catch block that swallows the error and returns status 'down' with no logging. While the 'down' fallback is correct, the actual error (connection refused vs. TLS error vs. auth failure) is completely invisible to the operator. This makes diagnosing gateway connectivity failures much harder. Note: gateway/client.ts:215 has an intentional 'Ignore non-JSON messages' catch — do NOT touch that one. Steps: (1) open src/gateway/health.ts; (2) find catch block at line 104; (3) add console.warn() logging the error before returning the 'down' status; (4) do NOT change the 'down' return value; (5) run gateway/ tests to confirm no regression. Scope: 1 one-line addition in one file.
